title: Airline station managers need a work permit - Canada.ca
content:
## You need a work permit
If you’re an airline station manager, you need a work permit to work in Canada.
You’re an intra-company transferee who will work temporarily in Canada.
- show that you’ll bring your expertise to the Canadian business
- show that the company in your home country and the Canadian company have a qualifying relationship
- meet the general eligibility requirements (opens in a new tab) for a work permit
You can apply for an employer-specific work permit (opens in a new tab) .
When you fill out your work permit application, select “Exemption from Labour Market Impact Assessment” as the type of work permit in the “Details of intended work in Canada” section.
- submit an offer of employment
- pay a $230 employer compliance fee
- give you an offer of employment number
To have a qualifying relationship, both the Canadian and the foreign company must be legal entities. They must  also have one of the following relationships:
Both the Canadian and the foreign company must be or will be doing business. Having an office or agent in Canada is not enough. The companies must be regularly, systematically and continuously providing goods or services through a parent, branch or affiliate in Canada and  the foreign country.

### Evidence of qualifying relationship
These documents can be submitted as  evidence of doing business:
- annual reports (for public companies)
- articles of incorporation
- profit/loss statements
- partnership agreements
- licence to do business
- business tax returns
- registration as an employer with the Canada Revenue Agency
You must also be able to show that  you’ll transfer back to the foreign company at the end of your assignment in Canada.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
